title: The Delivery (2)

jokes.text: She's 10 inches dilated now. Sorry, meters... Centimeters.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to sound medical/knowledgeable about labor progress
jokes.punchline: His complete confusion about basic units of measurement reveals his panic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim panic moment - the escalation from inches to meters to centimeters shows his complete disorientation. Character-specific and well-structured.

jokes.text: But no baby yet. It's only been six... Nineteen hours.
jokes.setup: Minimizing the time with 'only been six...'
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually been 19 hours, showing how exhausted he is
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure showing Jim's exhausted state through his attempt to downplay the time.

jokes.text: No, don't push. Pull! Pull!
jokes.setup: Jim trying to help during delivery
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstands childbirth mechanics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment showing Jim's complete panic and confusion. The idea of 'pulling' during childbirth is both absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Gotta go wash my eyes.
jokes.setup: Jim experiencing the reality of childbirth
jokes.punchline: His immediate need to 'wash his eyes' from what he's seen
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jim's squeamishness. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: That kid's gonna have a lot of hair.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on the newborn
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about baby's hair as if it's unusual
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor, nothing exceptional but gets a mild chuckle.

jokes.text: Have you guys seen her? She hasn't popped yet. What? Damn it! She was supposed to come out yesterday.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about the baby's arrival
jokes.punchline: Treating birth timing like a scheduled appointment that's running late
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on birth timing, treating it like a business appointment.

jokes.text: I decided to give Baby Halpert the newspaper from the day she was born. This frame set me back 55 bones. But she decided to take her sweet time, so now I have to switch it with today's paper.
jokes.setup: Dwight's thoughtful gesture of framing birth day newspaper
jokes.punchline: His frustration at having to change the newspaper due to delayed birth
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - well-intentioned but frustrated by logistics. The phrase '55 bones' and treating the baby like she's being inconsiderate is perfect.

jokes.text: I have an ice cream cake in the car. Oh, my God! Go! Go! Go! Are you insane?
jokes.setup: Phyllis casually mentioning ice cream cake
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic and urgency about the melting cake
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from calm to panic. The visual of everyone freaking out about melting cake is funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's right. It's a baby, see?
jokes.setup: Someone explaining what everyone is looking at
jokes.punchline: Stating the completely obvious as if it's revelatory
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan moment, mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Sir, you can't smoke that in here. Can't smoke anywhere these days.
jokes.setup: Someone being told not to smoke in hospital
jokes.punchline: Complaining about smoking restrictions as if it's unreasonable
jokes.explanation: Generic observational humor about smoking restrictions. Not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: No, let's let her rest. I'm sure there are still 12 of each.
jokes.setup: Pam wanting to count fingers and toes again
jokes.punchline: Jim's confident assertion there are 12 of each (wrong number)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim moment - his deadpan confidence while being completely wrong about basic anatomy. Shows his exhausted state perfectly.

jokes.text: Eleven pounds, four... She's seven pounds, two ounces, 18 inches. Seven pounds.
jokes.setup: Jim announcing baby's stats
jokes.punchline: Gets confused and changes the weight mid-announcement
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's continued exhausted confusion, but not as strong as other similar moments.

jokes.text: There is nothing I cannot diaper. Go ahead. Try to think of something. I dare you.
jokes.setup: Jim claiming to be a diapering master
jokes.punchline: His overconfident challenge about diapering anything
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing new parent overconfidence. The challenge format makes it quotable.

jokes.text: Love you. Love you as a friend.
jokes.setup: Michael saying goodbye to Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: His awkward clarification that it's platonic love
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael cringe - the awkward over-clarification makes it worse. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Again. No pants.
jokes.setup: Someone observing the baby
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation about baby being naked again
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about baby nudity. Mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That's so weird. She was saying it just before you got here.
jokes.setup: Baby appears to say 'Michael'
jokes.punchline: Convenient timing that makes Michael feel special
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment playing on Michael's need to feel important. Good character integration.

jokes.text: I searched everywhere, but I didn't find it. What I did find was mold, and lots of it.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his mission to find Pam's iPod
jokes.punchline: Instead finding mold and getting distracted by a bigger problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - gets distracted from simple task by something he perceives as more important. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: I also made plans to ask Jim where he bought his marvelous sheets.
jokes.setup: Dwight listing his accomplishments while staying at Jim's
jokes.punchline: Including the mundane goal of asking about sheets
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Dwight-like detail. The word 'marvelous' and treating sheet quality as important business is great character work.

jokes.text: The odds of them getting together were insurmountainable.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing Jim and Pam's relationship odds
jokes.punchline: Uses the wrong word (insurmountable + insurmountainable)
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight malapropism. The made-up word is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I made a family. I got these two together and I made a family. This man has a gift.
jokes.setup: Dwight taking credit for Jim and Pam's relationship
jokes.punchline: His grandiose self-assessment and third-person praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight delusion of grandeur. The third-person 'This man has a gift' is quintessential Dwight ego.

jokes.text: Okay, show of hands. Who wants to live in a world where Stanley has two lovers and you don't have any?
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to motivate people to find love
jokes.punchline: Using Stanley having two lovers as the motivating comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant motivational tactic using Stanley's love life as the bar. Great callback and perfectly constructed argument.

jokes.text: Plus-size models, maybe.
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming to know models
jokes.punchline: Someone deflating his claim by specifying plus-size
jokes.explanation: Cheap shot humor based on body shaming. Gets a reaction but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Torn scrotum. Still on the mend, so... Not good timing.
jokes.setup: Gabe explaining why he can't date
jokes.punchline: Oversharing extremely personal medical information
jokes.explanation: Classic Gabe oversharing moment. The casual delivery of shocking information is perfectly in character and gets big laughs.

jokes.text: Yes, I'm gonna ask out Erin. I'm just waiting for the stars to align. Literally. I have a small skylight in my bedroom, and I'd like for the moon to be visible.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his plan to ask out Erin
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation of 'stars aligning' with specific skylight requirements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy moment showing his overthinking and literal nature. The absurd specificity of needing moon visibility through his skylight is perfectly crafted character comedy.

jokes.text: I am going to fill that empty hole in your body with another person.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to be poetic about love
jokes.punchline: Extremely awkward and anatomically confusing metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly uncomfortable Dwight metaphor that sounds inappropriate. The awkwardness is the joke.

jokes.text: I can't. She's too strong.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to swaddle the newborn
jokes.punchline: Claiming the baby is too strong to wrap
jokes.explanation: Good new parent humor - the absurdity of a newborn being 'too strong' captures new parent helplessness.

jokes.text: Do you want to try it, Jim? Mmm-mmm. I think you're good. Doing a good job.
jokes.setup: Lactation consultant asking if Jim wants to try breastfeeding
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate and emphatic refusal
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Jim's quick backpedal and the consultant's diplomatic response.

jokes.text: Yeah, maybe it'll be good 'cause then she can, like, socialize with the other babies.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to find positives in baby going to nursery
jokes.punchline: Suggesting newborns need to socialize
jokes.explanation: Sweet absurdist moment showing Jim's attempt to rationalize. The idea of newborn socialization is endearingly naive.

jokes.text: You got to let the cookies cool before you pop them in your mouth.
jokes.setup: Dwight setting up Kevin and Erin
jokes.punchline: Treating Kevin like an impulsive child around cookies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how Dwight sees Kevin. The parental tone is funny.

jokes.text: Kevin has an enormous heart. Literally, he has an elephant heart. He had a transplant when he was 17. Had some problems... Really? No. Kevin doesn't have an elephant heart, but he is very sensitive,
jokes.setup: Michael explaining Kevin has an enormous heart
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake medical story followed by admission he made it up
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect structure. Michael's elaborate fake story followed by casual admission is brilliantly executed and very quotable.

jokes.text: Nice. She touched my shoulder.
jokes.setup: Erin briefly touching Kevin's shoulder
jokes.punchline: Kevin's overenthusiastic interpretation of minimal contact
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment showing his low bar for romantic success. Simple but effective character comedy.

jokes.text: Wow, she just shoves the nipple right in there.
jokes.setup: Jim watching another mother breastfeed
jokes.punchline: His crude observation about breastfeeding technique
jokes.explanation: Mildly cringe humor about breastfeeding. Gets a reaction but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Why was I hiding behind the plant? Well, in college I took a botany class, and there was lots of drama in that class. Uh... Kids would gossip about me, so I would eavesdrop on them by hiding behind different plants in the botany class.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he was hiding behind a plant
jokes.punchline: Elaborate backstory about hiding behind plants in botany class
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy spiral into unnecessary backstory. The absurdity of systematically hiding behind different plants in botany class is perfectly Andy.

jokes.text: And then I would jump out at them and confront them and be like, 'You think all I do is hide behind plants and spy on people? Busted.'
jokes.setup: Andy continuing his plant-hiding story
jokes.punchline: The irony of confronting people about thinking he hides behind plants while literally hiding behind plants
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the plant story with brilliant irony. Andy confronting people about the exact thing he's doing is comedic gold.

jokes.text: Got milk?
jokes.setup: Lactation consultant introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Using the famous milk advertising slogan
jokes.explanation: Cheap reference humor using obvious advertising slogan. Not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Nothing left to do except dot the I's, the J's and the umlauts.
jokes.setup: Dwight talking about contract details
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding the phrase 'dot the i's' by including J's and umlauts
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight malapropism showing his literal interpretation of idioms. The inclusion of umlauts is particularly funny.

jokes.text: Why don't you meet me here at exactly mid-late afternoon?
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to schedule a meeting
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical time designation 'mid-late afternoon'
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight precision that makes no sense. The contradiction of 'mid-late' is perfectly Dwight.

jokes.text: Oh! We're doing it. That's great. We're really parents now.
jokes.setup: Baby finally latching for breastfeeding
jokes.punchline: Pam deciding this moment makes them 'really parents'
jokes.explanation: Sweet new parent moment with the arbitrary milestone of 'now we're really parents.' Relatable humor.

jokes.text: Look, Kevin, do you really think that you could have dated Erin? You said she liked me. Okay, even if someone told you that, you should know that that could never be possible, Kevin.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to let Kevin down easy
jokes.punchline: His brutal honesty about Kevin's dating prospects
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael cruelty disguised as help. The blunt 'that could never be possible' is harsh but in character.

jokes.text: I've done better than Erin. No, you haven't. Lynn was way hotter than Erin, Michael. Lynn was as hot as Erin.
jokes.setup: Kevin defending his dating history
jokes.punchline: Michael's brutal corrections to Kevin's claims
jokes.explanation: Good escalating argument with Michael's immediate shutdowns. The negotiation down to 'as hot as' is funny.

jokes.text: Wrong baby. What? Wrong baby. This is not our baby!
jokes.setup: Isabel holding what they think is Jim and Pam's baby
jokes.punchline: Realization they've been caring for the wrong baby
jokes.explanation: Great panic moment with escalating horror. The simple 'Wrong baby' delivers maximum impact with economy.

jokes.text: Newsflash, the whole thing needs to go in the car.
jokes.setup: Nurse explaining car seat installation
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic response about obvious information
jokes.explanation: Typical new parent stress response. Mildly funny but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Are they, now? How some people spend their money, right?
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining adults should go to dentist
jokes.punchline: Isabel's response suggesting dental care is frivolous spending
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment showing class/priority differences. Subtle character work.

jokes.text: The fax says, 'Erin, will you have dinner with me? From, Andy.' And the number is our office fax number.
jokes.setup: Andy creating elaborate fax scheme
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of faxing from same office to ask someone out
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Andy overcomplicated approach to simple task. The absurdity of the same-office fax is great.

jokes.text: You can't talk to me like that. I didn't do anything wrong.
jokes.setup: Andy threatening to fire Erin over the fax
jokes.punchline: Erin's appropriate response to inappropriate workplace behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Erin standing up for herself. Creates uncomfortable workplace dynamic.

jokes.text: Five tickets on the windshield.
jokes.setup: Jim returning from moving the car
jokes.punchline: Multiple parking tickets as consequence of the car seat confusion
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier confusion with escalated consequences. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I think I'm going to be sick.
jokes.setup: Dwight reflecting on Erin and Kevin not working out
jokes.punchline: Physical reaction to his failed matchmaking
jokes.explanation: Shows Dwight's investment in his matchmaking 'success.' Simple reaction but in character.

jokes.text: I couldn't find your iPod. Give me a couple days. Be out of your hair.
jokes.setup: Dwight's original mission to find Pam's iPod
jokes.punchline: Admitting failure and asking for more time after elaborate home renovation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Dwight's mission creep. The understatement after renovating their kitchen is great.

tmdb_overview: When the sales staff lets Sabre's new "sales is king" policy get to their heads, Michael hides their new, prized leads.
